 articles

On Getting Started (again)

A good warm up gets you in the groove and prevents injuries

2023-01-12
Table of Contents

  • Tools
  • How it works
  • Off topic

In Tae-Kwon-Do, one is required to bend and contort their body in ways one doesn’t usually perform with 99% of their week. Unless you are performing hooking kicks on a daily basis, warm ups are essential to prevent injury. It is common knowledge in sports and athletics but what about creative endeavours?

Seldom do we talk about warming up before we start working on art / music / writing. I had the privielge of working with a few artists and I noticed they all had a warm up routine. It was a way to get into the groove and get the creative juices flowing. Yet as babes, we are expected to just “bang it out.”

Tools

I built www.pickoneatrandom.com exactly for this purpose. I hope it helps you as much as it has helped me to get into the groove. 7

  1. Hit “Add a new deck” and enter a name for the deck
  2. Hit “Add a new item” and enter a name for the item
  3. Add as many items as you want
  4. Go back to the main screen and voila! You get one thing random,

Note: it only gives you the random things ONCE per day. This means you can’t just keep hitting the button until you get what you want.

How it works

It was very important to me that the app be as simple as possible. I wanted my items to persist so I only had to do the work once and not have touch things again. With that in mind, I decided to forgo any real backend and just have the app store the data locally. Turns out this was a great idea because I was able to get the app up and running in a few hours and it works great!

Off topic

This was my first app I had ever written using Neovim. I had been using Vim for a few years and decided to give Neovim a try when I discovered LazyVim could basically salvage my absolutely terrible Vim configuration. And you know what? It worked! I am so surprised at how effortless it was all to set up and I keep my hands moving as much as I want. Some may say that Neovim isn’t a real IDE or whatever but for something as simple as this it works great.I had a lot of fun with this project.

＜
